hindiTranslation: सुहृद्, मित्र, वैरी, उदासीन, मध्यस्थ, द्वेष्य और सम्बन्धियोंमें तथा साधुआचरण करनेवालोंमें और पापआचरण करनेवालोंमें भी समबुद्धिवाला मनुष्य श्रेष्ठ है ॥
englishTranslation: The one whose understanding is equal toward friend, enemy, neutral person, mediator, hateful person, relative, the virtuous, and the sinful is श्रेष्ठ.
essence: Equal vision toward all people marks the highest steadiness.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A calm, even-minded person does not get pulled by labels like friend, enemy, good, or bad. That balanced understanding is considered best.
simpleInsight: You stop being manipulated by preference and aversion, and that gives real inner freedom.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Krishna keeps teaching Arjuna how a steady meditator sees the world. After describing mastery over the mind and senses, he says the highest balance is to look on all people without bias.
modernRelevance: A group chat splits into camps and everyone wants your reaction. You feel the pull to defend one side and condemn the other. This verse asks for a steadier mind that does not get recruited by emotion.
translationLiteral: सुहृद् (well-wisher) / मित्र (friend) / अरि (enemy) / उदासीन (indifferent) / मध्यस्थ (mediator) / द्वेष्य (hateful) / बन्धुषु (among relatives) / साधुषु (among the virtuous) / अपि (also) / च (and) / पापेषु (among the sinful) / समबुद्धिः (equal understanding) / विशिष्यते (is श्रेष्ठ)
insight: It trains you to respond from clear seeing, not from liking and disliking.

quoteVariants.text: See friend and enemy with the same mind. That steadiness is higher.
My mind is sharper when it stops choosing sides.
Hold everyone in one clear regard.
